summary_one_line: An attribute whose prefix is exactly 'xml' and whose local name is exactly 'id' (the xml:id attribute), so prefix==ctxt->str_xml.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element bearing an attribute literally named 'xml:id' (prefix bytes 'xml', local-name bytes 'id' followed by NUL), so the interned prefix pointer equals ctxt->str_xml and localname[0..2]=='i','d',0 at SAX2.c:2173-2175.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winner seeds contain the literal 'xml:id="' attribute token (Seed 2 bytes at 0x0e-0x15: 78 6d / 6c 3a 69 64 3d 22 = 'xm','l:id="'), exercising the xml:id true branch; the winner takes the true side T=13 F=0 at L2173 and reaches the xmlValidateNCName / xmlAddID body (lines 2181-2190 marked [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds carry DTD/ATTLIST and xmlns:xlink tokens (Seed 2/4: 'ATTLIST b xmlns:xlink', 'SYSTEM "dtds') but never the 'xml:id' attribute name, so at L2173 the loser is T=0 F=39 and falls through into the xmlIsID / xmlIsRef else-arms (L2191/L2193 [L]).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers share the same input-to-state substitution stage, so the single-technique delta is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. value_profile_cmplog gets Hamming/prefix-distance feedback on the byte comparisons that build the 'xml:id' attribute name (the prefix-interning string match and the localname[0]=='i', [1]=='d', [2]==0 char chain), rewarding partial-match intermediate inputs and climbing toward the exact xml:id token; plain cmplog lacks this gradient and must hit the multi-byte token by substitution / havoc alone, which it rarely completes within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the xml:id attribute gate: prefix must equal the interned 'xml' string.
The full gate additionally requires the local name to be exactly 'id' (i, d, NUL terminator).
A value_profile_cmplog winner seed contains the literal xml:id attribute token.
That winner seed was discovered by value_profile_cmplog.
The winner takes the xml:id true branch while the loser never does (T=13 F=0 vs T=0 F=39 at L2173).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2173  T=13 F=0  T=0 F=39  if ((prefix == ctxt->str_xml) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds carry DTD/ATTLIST and xmlns:xlink tokens, not an xml:id attribute.
The only technique difference between winner value_profile_cmplog and loser cmplog is value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog feedback adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's edge + CMP buffer signals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage (I2S-floor dash-row) verification is not the decisive signal here: the decisive delta is value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient), and BOTH involved fuzzers (cmplog loser, value_profile_cmplog winner) already carry I2SRandReplace, so an I2S-floor row would appear in both arms and cannot explain the divergence. The mechanism attributed is value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over the multi-byte xml:id token comparisons, which value_profile_cmplog has and cmplog lacks; this is not an I2SRandReplace claim so lineage dash-rows were not used to verify it.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an exact multi-byte 'xml:id' token where value_profile_cmplog shows no resolution advantage over cmplog (both resolve at the same rate), which would show the CMP_MAP gradient is not what climbs the token comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: The attribution rests on inferring that value_profile's gradient (not corpus/scheduling luck) is what lets vpc complete the multi-byte xml:id token, since both involved fuzzers share I2S and havoc and only an indirect CMP_MAP rationale separates them.
